title: Zeltnera calycosa
taxonomy: Zeltnera calycosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is a type of gentian, which is a diverse group of plants that are commonly found in mountainous regions. The plant is native to the western United States and is often found growing in moist areas such as meadows and woodlands. The classification of Zeltnera calycosa places it within the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. This classification reflects the plant's status as a flowering plant that belongs to the gentian family.
uses: Zeltnera calycosa, also known as the desert bluecurl, has been used in traditional medicine by various Native American tribes for its potential health benefits. Traditionally, the plant was used to treat ailments such as sore eyes, fever, and coughs. Its leaves and flowers are said to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help soothe skin irritations and wounds. Additionally, the plant's roots are believed to have been used to treat digestive issues, including diarrhea and constipation. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Zeltnera calycosa for medicinal purposes.

intro: Zeltnera calycosa, also known as spotted gilia or spotted gilia bee plant, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory problems due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Zeltnera calycosa contains bioactive constituents like alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory issues.
